macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Stock Markets Decline Amid Oil Volatility and Key Support Breaks
items.summary: The S&P 500 closed at 6,830.71, down 0.56% or 38.79 points, while the Dow fell 1.61% to 47,954.74 and Nasdaq dropped 0.26% to 22,748.99; markets wobbled below the 6900 pivot with the 100-day moving average breached, eyeing further downside to 6600 amid rising oil prices.[1][2]

items.title: European and Asian Markets Open Lower
items.summary: European DAX closed down 1.61% at 23,815.75; Asian indices opened weaker with Shanghai Composite down 0.55%, Nikkei down 1.09%, and S&P/ASX 200 down 1.22%.[2]

items.title: Plug Power Celebrates 2025 Results and New CEO with Nasdaq Closing Bell
items.summary: Plug Power (PLUG) will ring the Nasdaq Closing Bell on March 6, 2026, honoring its 12.9% revenue growth to ~$710 million in 2025 and new CEO Jose Luis Crespo.[6]

items.title: GE Healthcare Completes Double Top Pattern
items.summary: GE Healthcare Technologies (GEHC), an S&P 100 stock, moved below $76.66, completing a double top pattern; CF Industries (CF) broke above $110.52.[5]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: U.S. CLARITY Act Stalls Over Stablecoin Rewards Dispute
items.summary: Landmark crypto legislation has hit a new impasse as banks rejected a White House compromise on stablecoin reward provisions, citing concerns that yield-bearing products could trigger deposit flight. President Trump criticized the banking industry for attempting to undermine the crypto agenda.

items.title: $26.8 Billion in Crypto Options Expire, Potential Market Volatility Expected
items.summary: A significant $26.8 billion in cryptocurrency options expired on March 6, including 32,000 Bitcoin and 184,000 Ethereum options, which could lead to increased volatility as traders adjust positions.

items.title: South Korea Enforces Exchange Monopoly Crackdown with Three-Year Restructuring Deadline
items.summary: East Asian lawmakers are forcing major trading platforms like Upbit and Bithumb, which control roughly 90% of the local market, to overhaul ownership structures within three years, triggering liquidity migration among investors.

items.title: Institutional Adoption and Infrastructure Development Drive Crypto Market Focus
items.summary: Market participants are prioritizing institutional flows, infrastructure expansion for settlements and payment rails, and regulatory clarity as key drivers for long-term digital asset market growth and stability.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Groundbreaking Large Genome Model Trained on Trillions of DNA Bases
items.summary: Ars Technica reports on an open-source AI model revolutionizing bioinformatics by accurately identifying genes, regulatory sequences, and splice sites in vast genetic datasets. This advancement highlights AI's growing role in genomic modeling and software development.

items.title: NCSC Releases Joint Guidance on AI and ML Supply Chain Risks
items.summary: New guidance published on March 6, 2026, identifies unique supply chain vulnerabilities in AI systems, including data, models, software, and hardware, and provides mitigations for cybersecurity resilience.

items.title: China's AI Breakthroughs Challenge US Technological Dominance
items.summary: Newsweek covers how recent Chinese AI advancements are intensifying global competition, as highlighted in the March 6, 2026, issue amid discussions of US AI stock bubbles and leadership shifts.

items.title: 62% of UK Executives Rely on AI for Most Decisions
items.summary: A survey of 200 UK business leaders reveals heavy dependence on LLMs for decision-making, with 70% second-guessing their own judgments when conflicting with AI recommendations, raising concerns about critical thinking.

items.title: AI Strategy in 2026 Demands Long-Term Commitment Over Timing
items.summary: Analysis argues AI success requires embedding learning loops for compounding performance gains, citing examples like Ferrari's data-driven services and warning that 97% of pilots fail without structural integration.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Trump Administration Considers Restricting Global Chip Shipments
items.summary: The Trump administration is considering a proposed regulation that would require US approval for virtually all exports of AI accelerators from companies like NVIDIA and AMD, expanding current curbs that cover around 40 countries.

items.title: Tech Companies Sign Ratepayer Protection Pledge
items.summary: America's largest tech companies officially signed the Ratepayer Protection Pledge on March 5, 2026.

items.title: Elevated Oil and Chip Concerns Weigh on Asian Stocks
items.summary: Asian stock markets experienced declines as elevated oil prices and semiconductor supply concerns, including potential chip export restrictions, impacted investor sentiment.
